zh.Witchingham, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可維其加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Germain de Berney於大約1273年出生在英國諾福爾可維其加姆時，他的父親Henry de Berney34歲，母親Catherine de Harsyk30歲。他於1291年與Alice Deedgefield在英國諾福爾可維其加姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1325年在英國諾福爾可諾維其享年54歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可諾維其。

zh-hans.Witchingham, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可維其加姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Germain de Berney于大约1273年出生在英国諾福爾可維其加姆时，他的父亲Henry de Berney34岁，母亲Catherine de Harsyk30岁。他于1291年与Alice Deedgefield在英国諾福爾可維其加姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1325年在英国諾福爾可諾維其享年54岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: Berney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Biorna ‘son of Biorna’. See Burney 2 and McBurney .
French: from a derivative of the personal name Bernard or any other name composed of the ancient Germanic element ber(n) ‘bear’.

variants.name: Burney
meanings: English (northern, of Norman origin): habitational name from Bernay in Eure, France, named with a Gaulish personal name Brenno + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish (Antrim) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Biorna ‘son of Biorna’, a Gaelic form of the Old Norse personal name Bjarni (from björn ‘bear cub, warrior’).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Germain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Germain de Berney was born about 1273, in Witchingham, Norfolk, England, his father, Henry de Berney, was 34 and his mother, Catherine de Harsyk, was 30. He married Alice Deedgefield in 1291, in Witchingham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1325, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Germain de Berney est né environ 1273, à Witchingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Berney, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Catherine de Harsyk, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Alice Deedgefield en 1291, à Witchingham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1325, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Germain de Berney um 1273 in Witchingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Berney 34 und seine Mutter Catherine de Harsyk 30. Er hat 1291 in Witchingham, Norfolk, England Alice Deedgefield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1325 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Norwich, Norfolk, England und wurde in Norwich, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Germain de Berney è nato il circa 1273 nel Witchingham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Henry de Berney, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Catherine de Harsyk, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Alice Deedgefield nel 1291, nel Witchingham, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1325, nel Norwich, Norfolk, England, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Norwich, Norfolk, England.

ja.Witchingham, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクウィトチンガム
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Germain de Berneyが約1273年にイギリスノーフォルクウィトチンガムで生まれた時，父Henry de Berneyは34歳，母Catherine de Harsykは30歳であった。1291年にイギリスノーフォルクウィトチンガムでAlice Deedgefieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1325年にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで54歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクノーウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Germain de Berney(이)가 대략 1273년 영국 노르폴크 윗친그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Berney(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine de Harsyk(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1291년 영국 노르폴크 윗친그함에서 Alice Deedgefield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1325년 영국 노르폴크 노위치에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1273–about 1325: tany ho any amin'ny 1273–tany ho any amin'ny 1325

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Germain de Berney nasceu aproximadamente 1273, em Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Berney, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Catherine de Harsyk, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Deedgefield em 1291, em Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1325, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Germain de Berney родился около 1273 г. в местности вичингхам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Berney было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine de Harsyk, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Deedgefield в 1291 г. в местности вичингхам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1325 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия.

sm.about 1273–about 1325: e tusa o le 1273–e tusa o le 1325

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Germain de Berney nació aproximadamente 1273, en Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Berney, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Catherine de Harsyk, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Alice Deedgefield en 1291, en Witchingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1325, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
